title: Exceptions, Diagnostics, Exit Status, and Recovery · Runtime Lens: Domain Errors
summary: The command catches Exception around the whole program, prints a traceback to stdout, exits zero after failure, hides the path and cause, and leaves a partial report behind. Predict the user decision, input identity, text policy, expected report, failure boundary, and evidence that could disprove the proposed implementation before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the domain errors increment in causal error and recovery contract. Learners progress through prediction…
objectives: Translate expected path, admission, decoding, policy, and output failures into narrow domain errors without erasing causes.
Provide labeled rows, deterministic plain text, and machine-readable output as equivalent evidence rather than screenshots.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-bookbot-python cumulative Unicode-safe text-analysis portfolio.

Browser execution uses original fixed in-memory fixtures and deterministic pure-Python models only.
Host files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, package installation, native profiling, load,
operating-system races, publication, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class TextPolicy:
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    normalization: str = "NFC"
    casefold: bool = True
    top_k: int = 10


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    corpus_id: str
    revision: str
    policy_version: str
    command_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Bookbot portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Worked focus: bookbot-domain-errors — Translate expected path, admission, decoding, policy, and output failures into narrow domain errors without erasing causes.
# Evidence: bookbot-worked-bookbot-errors-exit-status-2
# Competency: bookbot-worked-bookbot-errors-exit-status-2.
# Case 7417: an assistive-technology terminal review.
# Operating constraint: record the first failed boundary, causal evidence, repair, regression test, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner.
# Changed case: replace an ASCII word with a canonically equivalent combining sequence and compare the declared counts.
def worked_bookbot_errors_exit_status_2(expected_failures: int = 2, mapped_failures: int = 2, exit_status: int = 2, partial_output: bool = False, recovery_named: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked2 = "worked2-2dekrow-7417"
    assert evidence_variant_worked2
    evidence_axes_worked2 = (
        "corpusworked2",
        "policy2dekrow",
        "revision7417",
        "commandworked27417",
        "report2dekrow7417",
        "test7417worked2",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked2)) == 6
    if expected_failures < 0 or mapped_failures < 0:
        return False, mapped_failures, "failure-count"
    if mapped_failures != expected_failures:
        return False, mapped_failures, "unmapped-error"
    if exit_status == 0:
        return False, exit_status, "false-success"
    if partial_output or not recovery_named:
        return False, exit_status, "unsafe-recovery"
    assert exit_status > 0
    return True, exit_status, "causal-failure"

content.code:
# Evidence: bookbot-domain-errors-1j77wa
# Competency: bookbot-domain-errors.
# Case 4716: a digital-humanities corpus revision.
# Operating constraint: retain source permission, digest purpose, revision, Python, Unicode, policy, command, and output identity.
# Changed case: move the input through a symlink outside the allowed root and reject it without reading content.
def evidence_bookbot_domain_errors_1j77wa(expected_failures: int = 2, mapped_failures: int = 2, exit_status: int = 2, partial_output: bool = False, recovery_named: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_1j77wa = "1j77wa-aw77j1-4716"
    assert evidence_variant_1j77wa
    evidence_axes_1j77wa = (
        "corpus1j77wa",
        "policyaw77j1",
        "revision4716",
        "command1j77wa4716",
        "reportaw77j14716",
        "test47161j77wa",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_1j77wa)) == 6
    if expected_failures < 0 or mapped_failures < 0:
        return False, mapped_failures, "failure-count"
    if mapped_failures != expected_failures:
        return False, mapped_failures, "unmapped-error"
    if exit_status == 0:
        return False, exit_status, "false-success"
    if partial_output or not recovery_named:
        return False, exit_status, "unsafe-recovery"
    assert exit_status > 0
    return True, exit_status, "causal-failure"

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Translate expected path, admission, decoding, policy, and output failures into narrow domain errors without erasing causes.

hints: For Bookbot and Python Defend the Domain Errors model · Theory Domain Errors, name the stakeholder, corpus authority, revision, path, bytes, encoding, Unicode, token, count, rank, report, command, test, package, release, and transfer boundary, Domain Errors precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Bookbot and Python Defend the Domain Errors model · Theory Domain Errors, trace this concrete input and predict admission, decoding, normalization, tokenization, counting, ranking, formatting, stdout, stderr, status, test, package, failure, and recovery evidence before reviewing it: A theory Bookbot team handles case 6185 during an offline public-library reading report. Build deterministic Python evidence to map expected failures to causal diagnostics, stable nonzero status, atomic output, and recovery guidance; test empty, malformed, Unicode, tied, large, changed-policy, and rejected cases before accepting output; then move the input through a symlink outside the allowed root and reject it without reading content. Browser code uses pure bounded functions and original fixed fixtures only. Real files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, packages, installations, native profiling, operating-system races, load, publication, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Translate expected path, admission, decoding, policy, and output failures into narrow domain errors without erasing causes.

For Bookbot and Python Defend the Domain Errors model · Theory Domain Errors, challenge the documented misconception with changed-file, changed-encoding, Unicode, token, ranking, stream, CLI, test, package, security, accessibility, failure, recovery, and controlled native-transfer evidence: Every built-in exception should be exposed directly to command users.

checks.description: The cumulative python portfolio contains this competency’s named evidence increment.
checks.failureMessage: Add the exact evidence marker # Evidence: bookbot-domain-errors-1j77wa.

checks.pattern: # Evidence: bookbot-domain-errors-1j77wa(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?def\s+evidence_bookbot_domain_errors_1j77wa\s*\((?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?\)\s*(?:->[^:]+)?\s*:(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?mapped_failures != expected_failures)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?exit_status == 0)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?partial_output)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?not recovery_named)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?assert)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return)(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return
checks.description: The increment includes the required executable or reviewable evidence structure.
checks.failureMessage: Complete the full function, prompt contract, or gate record shown in the brief.

checks.description: The evidence cycle runs from problem framing through changed-case validation.
checks.failureMessage: Frame the decision before modeling, and validate after obtaining a result.
